title: REVOLUTION 2026 SPRING MEN'S LEAGUE | WEEK 8 RECAP
content: Week 8 was one for the books. Purple walked into the biggest game of the season and handled the undefeated juggernaut Green, their first loss of the year. Beyond that seismic result, Hakeem Faud went for 47 in a losing effort for Tan, White rallied from five down at halftime to edge Pink by one, Gray erased an 11-point deficit to knock off Yellow, and Orange got another Trent Roleau masterclass to fend off Blue. Let's get into the action.

games.text: Pink held the lead in the first. Pink came out sharp and built a five-point cushion heading into the break. Tyrone Conley was the engine for Pink, attacking from every angle and knocking down six threes. Liam Walsh controlled the paint, and Kyle Haley stayed active on both ends. White found themselves chasing, but the deficit felt manageable. Halftime: Pink 46, White 41. White came out of halftime in an entirely different gear. Tavian Barrino stepped up in a big moment, finishing with 23 points on 6-of-7 from the free-throw line to go with 4 rebounds. Zaire Wilson added 20 points and 12 rebounds. Donnie Dwyer stayed steady with 21 points. This one came down to the wire with both teams scrapping back and forth. White had the ball out of bounds with five seconds left on the clock. Zaire Wilson hoisted a desperation shot that got fumbled around by multiple players and found its way into Donnie Dwyer's hands, who released the ball just in the nick of time to put White on top. White won, 83-82.

performers.White: Tavian Barrino — 23 pts, 4 REB, 1 AST; Donnie Dwyer — 21 pts, 3 REB, 2 AST; Zaire Wilson — 20 pts, 12 REB, 5 AST
performers.Pink: Tyrone Conley — 26 pts, 11 REB, 6 AST; Liam Walsh — 23 pts, 8 REB, 3 AST; Kyle Haley — 18 pts, 7 REB, 5 AST

games.text: Hakeem Faud gave Black everything he had, but it was not enough. Faud was a singular force all morning, putting together one of the most remarkable individual performances of the season with 47 points on 22-of-42 shooting with 18 rebounds. But Black is a team that can absorb a 47-point individual game from the opposition and still win, and that is exactly what happened. Black jumped out to a 13-point halftime lead and never relinquished it. Halftime: Black 50, Tan 37. Black stayed disciplined in the second half while Faud kept working for Tan. Stefan Radan finished with 30 points and 18 rebounds in a complete performance, while Chris Cayole added 29 points and a remarkable 20 rebounds. Tanner Freeman ran the offense with 12 points and 6 assists. For Tan, Devin Bradford chipped in 12 points, but with almost no other support for Faud, the outcome was inevitable. Black won going away. Black won, 91-65.

performers.Black: Stefan Radan — 30 pts, 18 REB, 4 AST; Chris Cayole — 29 pts, 20 REB, 4 AST; Kenny Black — 12 pts, 12 REB, 5 AST
performers.Tan: Hakeem Faud — 47 pts, 18 REB, 1 AST; Devin Bradford — 12 pts, 4 REB, 2 AST; Sub Brandon — 4 pts, 1 REB

games.text: Trent Roleau was at it again, and Orange made just enough plays to hold on. Roleau steadily took over the game by getting to his spots and converting at a high clip. Will Oboh and Walker Storey gave Orange secondary options that Blue couldn't fully contain. But Blue kept battling behind Jackson McCoy, who was tireless all game. Halftime: Orange 34, Blue 31. The second half was a dead heat, with both teams matching each other basket for basket. McCoy continued to assert himself offensively, while James Hare and Nicky Lynn provided timely baskets as the clock ticked down. The score was knotted at 76 with less than 30 seconds to play, and Orange had possession. As the shot clock wound down, Will Oboh connected on an absolute bomb to beat the shot clock buzzer and put Orange ahead. Blue was unable to match that bucket, and Orange walked away victorious. Orange won, 79-76.

performers.Orange: Trent Roleau — 34 pts, 14 REB, 1 AST; Will Oboh — 15 pts, 8 REB, 5 AST; Walker Storey — 13 pts, 10 REB, 5 AST
performers.Blue: Jack McCoy — 29 pts, 16 REB, 4 AST; Nick Lynn — 12 pts, 7 REB, 1 AST; James Hare — 10 pts, 5 REB, 4 AST

games.text: This game was a tale of two halves. Yellow played their best first half of the season, building a comfortable lead on the strength of Logan Letourneau and Alex Philo's shooting. Drew Pelkey controlled the glass, as Gray looked out of sorts on offense and couldn't find any rhythm in the first half. Halftime: Yellow 35, Gray 24. Then Kevin Garrison decided the game was over. Garrison willed Gray back into it and then past Yellow entirely, finishing with 31 points on 11-of-24 shooting, 4 threes, and 11 rebounds with 3 assists. Kyle DeCoster hit some big shots to go with 15 points, and Dylan Pratico added 14. Gray outscored Yellow 45-28 in the second half to complete one of the more impressive comebacks of the season. Gray won, 69-63.

performers.Gray: Kevin Garrison — 31 pts, 11 REB, 3 AST; Kyle DeCoster — 15 pts, 4 REB, 1 AST; Dylan Pratico — 14 pts, 4 REB, 3 AST
performers.Yellow: Logan Letourneau — 17 pts, 11 REB; Alex Philo — 16 pts, 5 REB; Drew Pelkey — 11 pts, 13 REB, 4 AST

games.text: Green came in undefeated and left with their first loss of the season. Purple built a quick cushion behind some hot shooting and great team basketball. Tre Grubbs was spectacular, shooting 5-of-6 from three and going a perfect 4-of-4 from the free-throw line on his way to 25 points. Trevis Buckhannon dominated the glass with 16 rebounds and added 18 points, while Kaleb Swetty hit four threes and scored 23 to give Purple a third reliable option. Halftime: Purple 48, Green 44. Purple came out in the second half and buried Green with a 50-37 frame that was never really in doubt. For Green, Mahlik Franklin finished with 37 points and 9 rebounds in a heroic effort, while Jaren Jeffcoat added 14. Purple's depth and balance were simply too much on this day. Green's undefeated run is over, but their lead in the Eastern Conference remains intact. Meanwhile, Purple put the rest of the Western Conference on notice. Purple won, 98-81.

performers.Purple: Tre Grubbs — 25 pts, 11 REB, 4 AST; Kaleb Swetty — 23 pts, 6 REB, 5 AST; Trevis Buckhannon — 18 pts, 16 REB, 2 AST
performers.Green: Mahlik Franklin — 37 pts, 9 REB, 4 AST; Jaren Jeffcoat — 14 pts, 8 REB, 3 AST; Jerrick Jacobs — 13 pts, 2 REB

sponsors.name: Alta Group & The Windjammer Restaurant and Upper Deck Pub
sponsors.message: A huge thank you to the Alta Group, The Windjammer Restaurant, and Upper Deck Pub for their continued support of the Revolution Men's League. None of this happens without you.

sponsors.name: Warden's Wisdom
sponsors.message: This recap is brought to you by Warden's Wisdom — a mindfulness-based coaching business dedicated to helping people relieve anxiety through personalized mindfulness practices. Whether you're defending a perfect record or chasing one, staying present is what gets you through.
sponsors.quote: No streak lasts forever. What matters is how you respond when the run ends.

closing: Green falls for the first time. The race is on. Week 9 cannot come soon enough. Tune in.
